executiveSummary.overview: Comprehensive test coverage plan for artifact tree integrity validation. Plan addresses all discovery findings: orphan artifact buffering, null output handling, event persistence, and content-hash deduplication. Test matrix covers 6 primary validation areas with 24+ specific test cases.
executiveSummary.criticality: HIGH - Prevents regressions in event-driven artifact persistence architecture

area1_PropagationArtifactEmission.purpose: Verify that propagation events emit artifacts correctly and establish proper parent-child relationships

testCases.description: Verify that a basic propagation execution emits PropagationEvent with correct artifact payload
steps: Create a test propagator with simple text transformation
Assert PropagationEvent is published to EventBus
Verify event contains correct artifact key and parent reference
testCases.expectedResult: PropagationEvent emitted with artifact payload containing executionKey, parentKey, and proper artifact type
validationCheckpoints: Event payload contains executionKey matching execution context
Event payload contains parentKey linking to source artifact

testCases.description: Verify that emitted propagation artifacts are persisted to ArtifactEntity table
steps: Execute propagation and capture emitted event
Allow listener to process event and trigger persistence
Query ArtifactRepository for artifact with emitted key
Verify entity contents match event payload
testCases.expectedResult: ArtifactEntity row created with correct artifactKey, contentHash, and parent-child linkage
validationCheckpoints: contentHash matches SHA-256 of artifact content
Indices on artifact_key, parent_key, execution_key populated correctly

testCases.description: Verify that duplicate propagations (same input, same propagator) create only one PropagationRecordEntity
preconditions: Same propagation executed twice with identical inputs
steps: Execute propagation with input A and propagator P
Execute same propagation again (same input, same propagator)
testCases.expectedResult: correlationKey remains consistent; database constraint prevents duplicate records
validationCheckpoints: Database unique constraint on (registrationId, layerId, correlationKey) enforced
Second execution does not create duplicate record

testCases.description: Verify that single propagation execution can emit multiple artifacts (e.g., batched propagations)
preconditions: Propagator configured to emit multiple outputs
steps: Create propagator that generates 5 artifacts in single execution
Verify all artifacts persisted to database
testCases.expectedResult: All 5 artifacts emitted as separate events and persisted with correct parent linkage
validationCheckpoints: All events contain unique artifactKey values

area2_FilterArtifactEmission.areaName: Filter Artifact Emission and Decision Persistence
area2_FilterArtifactEmission.purpose: Verify that filter operations emit filtered artifacts and persist decision records WITHOUT emitting FilterEvent

testCases.description: Verify that filters emit artifacts but do NOT publish FilterEvent to EventBus (architectural difference from propagators)
steps: Monitor EventBus for all event publications
testCases.expectedResult: Filter artifacts persisted via decision records without event emission
validationCheckpoints: No FilterEvent found in EventBus publication log
Filtered artifact available via artifact query (not via event)

testCases.description: Verify all filter decision action values (ACCEPTED, REJECTED, ERROR, PASSTHROUGH) persisted correctly
preconditions: Filter configured with multiple decision branches
Test data covering all action paths
steps: Execute filter with data triggering ACCEPTED action
Execute filter with data triggering REJECTED action
Execute filter with error condition triggering ERROR action
testCases.expectedResult: All action types persisted with correct counts and context
validationCheckpoints: FilterDecisionRecordEntity rows for each action type present
sourceNodeId correlates correctly with filtered stream element

testCases.description: Verify filtered artifacts maintain correct parent linkage to stream source
steps: Execute filter on stream with known source artifact
Verify parentKey in ArtifactEntity matches stream source
testCases.expectedResult: All filtered artifacts linked to stream source as parent
validationCheckpoints: Associated ArtifactEntity.parentKey matches stream source key
Artifact tree hierarchy preserves filter source relationship

area3_TransformerParenting.purpose: Verify that transformations establish correct artifact parent-child relationships in trie structure

testCases.description: Verify transformer establishes parent-child relationship: input artifact → transformation artifact
steps: Verify transformation emits artifact with parentKey = source.artifactKey
testCases.expectedResult: Child artifact correctly parented to source artifact
validationCheckpoints: Tree traversal from child to parent follows correct path

testCases.description: Verify multiple chained transformations maintain correct lineage: A → B → C
preconditions: Transformer T2 can reference B (output of T1)
steps: Execute T1(A) → produces artifact B with parentKey=A
Execute T2(B) → produces artifact C with parentKey=B
testCases.expectedResult: Lineage chain C←B←A persisted correctly with depths 2-1-0
validationCheckpoints: Tree traversal from C to root follows A→B→C path

testCases.description: Verify transformer producing multiple outputs all have same parent
preconditions: Transformer configured to produce multiple outputs
steps: Execute transformer producing 3 artifacts from single source
Verify all 3 artifacts have parentKey = source.artifactKey
Query artifacts by parentKey and verify count
testCases.expectedResult: All child artifacts share single parent

area4_NullOutputHandling.purpose: Verify all execution services gracefully handle null outputs and log appropriately

testCases.description: Verify propagation handles null output gracefully without artifact emission
steps: Verify no ArtifactEntity created for null output
testCases.expectedResult: Null output converted to failedOutput() with proper action status
validationCheckpoints: No ArtifactEntity.artifactKey found for null payload
Log contains explicit log entry: 'Null output from propagator'
PropagationEvent emitted with failed action status

testCases.description: Verify transformer handles null output gracefully
steps: Verify TransformationRecordEntity persisted with error status
testCases.expectedResult: Null output results in error record without artifact creation
validationCheckpoints: No ArtifactEntity created for null output
errorMessage field populated with null indicator
TransformationEvent emitted (if event emitted) with error status

testCases.description: Verify filter handles null output by treating as passthrough
testCases.expectedResult: Null in filter context results in passthrough decision

testCases.description: Verify that null outputs do not cause orphan artifact buffering issues
testCases.expectedResult: Null outputs do not corrupt orphan buffer state
validationCheckpoints: No artifacts left in buffer after execution

area5_OrphanArtifactBuffering.areaName: Orphan Artifact Buffering and Parent Linking
area5_OrphanArtifactBuffering.purpose: Verify orphan artifacts are buffered correctly and linked when parents are established

testCases.description: Verify artifact is buffered when parent doesn't exist in tree
preconditions: Parent artifact has not been added yet
steps: Attempt to add artifact with parentKey = non-existent-key
Verify artifact added to orphan buffer
Query orphan buffer for buffered artifact
testCases.expectedResult: Orphan artifact stored in buffer awaiting parent
validationCheckpoints: Artifact not yet in main tree structure

testCases.description: Verify buffered orphan is linked when parent artifact is added to tree
preconditions: Parent artifact not yet in tree
steps: Verify orphan buffer scanned for matching parentKey
Verify orphaned artifact automatically linked to parent
Verify artifact node state changed from ORPHANED to LINKED
testCases.expectedResult: Buffered orphan automatically promoted to tree position
validationCheckpoints: Artifact now present in tree under parent node
Artifact.depth recalculated based on parent depth
Tree traversal from orphan to parent/root works correctly

testCases.description: Verify artifacts can arrive out of order and be correctly linked
preconditions: Multiple artifact levels: A (root) → B (child of A) → C (child of B)
steps: Add C with parentKey=B (orphan, B doesn't exist)
Add B with parentKey=A (orphan, A doesn't exist)
Add A with no parent (root, added to tree)
Verify chain auto-linking in order: A → B → C
testCases.expectedResult: Complete lineage established despite out-of-order arrival
validationCheckpoints: After adding A: both B and C still buffered (A added but B's parent link not yet verified)
After adding B: C automatically linked to B if B now has parent A
Final tree: C under B under A

testCases.description: Verify linked orphan artifact persisted to database correctly
steps: Verify all fields match tree state
testCases.expectedResult: Linked artifact persisted with correct parent/depth values
validationCheckpoints: parentKey matches parent artifact in database

testCases.description: Verify orphan buffer cleared or finalized at execution end
preconditions: Execution completed with some orphans (expected) and some linked
Orphan buffer contains artifacts with missing parents
steps: Verify still-orphaned artifacts either: (a) retained for next execution, or (b) marked for cleanup
testCases.expectedResult: Orphan buffer managed appropriately post-execution
validationCheckpoints: No memory leaks from accumulated orphans
Next execution starts with clean or properly initialized buffer

area6_ContentHashDeduplication.purpose: Verify SHA-256 content hash deduplication prevents duplicate artifacts at scale

testCases.description: Verify artifact with identical content to sibling is detected as duplicate in tree
preconditions: First child artifact added with content X
steps: Add first artifact with content X and parent P
Add second artifact with identical content X and parent P
testCases.expectedResult: Duplicate content detected at tree level, second artifact rejected
validationCheckpoints: Second artifact not added to tree
Only one artifact with contentHash exists under parent
Both computations of SHA-256(X) yield identical hash

testCases.description: Verify database constraint enforces content hash uniqueness
preconditions: First artifact with content X persisted with contentHash H
Unique constraint on contentHash column active
steps: Attempt to insert second ArtifactEntity with identical contentHash H
testCases.expectedResult: Database constraint prevents duplicate content hashes

testCases.description: Verify identical content in different executions detected as duplicate
preconditions: Execution 1 produces artifact with content X
steps: Execute E1, produces artifact A1 with content X
Persist A1 to database with contentHash H
Execute E2, produces artifact A2 with identical content X
testCases.expectedResult: Cross-execution duplicate detected and prevented
validationCheckpoints: ContentHash computation yields H for both A1 and A2
A2 persistence rejected by uniqueness constraint
Both executions reference same contentHash in metadata

testCases.description: Verify SHA-256 collision resistance with varied content
preconditions: Multiple artifacts with similar but distinct content
steps: Create 100+ artifacts with similar structures but different data
Attempt variations that might trigger collisions
testCases.expectedResult: SHA-256 provides sufficient differentiation for all variants
validationCheckpoints: Minor content changes produce completely different hashes
No collisions detected across test set
Hash computation deterministic (same content = same hash)

testCases.description: Verify deduplication reduces storage for repeated content
steps: With deduplication: verify only 1 row stored
testCases.expectedResult: Deduplication reduces storage by ~90% for repeated content
validationCheckpoints: Query by contentHash returns single artifact

testExecutionPhases.name: Unit Tests
testExecutionPhases.scope: Individual service methods and tree operations
testExecutionPhases.coverage: Core logic paths, edge cases, null handling

testExecutionPhases.name: Integration Tests
testExecutionPhases.coverage: Event-driven flows, artifact tree operations, database persistence

testExecutionPhases.name: End-to-End Tests
testExecutionPhases.scope: Complete workflows: execution → event → artifact → persistence
testExecutionPhases.coverage: All discovery areas, complete data flow validation

testExecutionPhases.name: Performance Tests
testExecutionPhases.coverage: 1000+ artifact operations, tree performance, persistence throughput

propagationTestData: Propagations with duplicate inputs (correlation key testing)
orphanTestData: Deep chains (3+ levels out of order)
Mixed order with some in-order arrivals

artifactEntityChecks: All emitted artifacts appear in database
propagationRecordChecks: Record created for each propagation execution
transformationRecordChecks: Action status and error handling logged

emissionTests: PropagationEvent published exactly once per execution
eventOrderingTests: Parent artifact event precedes child event
Event order matches logical execution order
No race conditions in event publishing
eventBuslistenerTests: Listener error handling doesn't block event flow

implementationSteps.description: Set up test fixtures, database setup/teardown, event bus mocking
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: Test harness ready for writing specific test cases

implementationSteps.description: Write 4 test cases for propagation artifact emission, persistence, deduplication, and batch handling
Write 3 test cases for filter emission (no event), decision actions, and stream parenting
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: Filter layer tested with confirmation of no-event design

implementationSteps.description: Write 3 test cases for parent-child linkage, chaining, and multi-output scenarios
Write 4 test cases covering null handling in all services and orphan buffer stability
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: Null output edge cases fully validated

implementationSteps.description: Write 5 test cases for orphan detection, buffering, linking, out-of-order arrival, and cleanup
Write 5 test cases for content hash detection, database constraints, cross-execution dedup, collision resistance, and space efficiency
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: Content-hash deduplication verified at tree and database levels

implementationSteps.description: End-to-end tests combining multiple test areas to validate complete workflows
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: Complete data flow validated from trigger through persistence

implementationSteps.description: Test with 1000+ artifacts, measure tree performance, verify deduplication efficiency
Analyze test coverage reports, identify gaps, fill missing coverage
implementationSteps.expectedOutcome: All critical paths covered to target percentages (90%+)

nextPhase.goal: Execute the comprehensive test plan and validate all artifact tree integrity requirements
successCriteria: All 24 test cases implemented and passing
Code coverage targets met (90%+ for all critical paths)
All discovery findings validated through tests

planningConclusion.summary: Comprehensive test coverage plan developed addressing all 6 validation areas with 24 specific, actionable test cases. Plan covers artifact emission (propagation, filter, transformer), parent-child relationships, null output handling, orphan buffering, and deduplication. Implementation follows 10-step progression from test infrastructure through performance validation. Ready for implementation phase.
